message.content: Configure my statusLine from my shell PS1 configuration

content.text: I'll read your shell configuration files to find your PS1 setting.

content.content:
1	# Enable Powerlevel10k instant prompt. Should stay close to the top of ~/.zshrc.
2	# Initialization code that may require console input (password prompts, [y/n]
3	# confirmations, etc.) must go above this block; everything else may go below.
4	if [[ -r "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh" ]]; then
5	  source "${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/p10k-instant-prompt-${(%):-%n}.zsh"
6	fi
7	
8	# Compile + run: cppr main.cpp [program args...]
9	cppr() {
10	  if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
11	    echo "usage: cppr <file.cpp> [args...]"
12	    return 2
13	  fi
14	
15	  local src="$1"; shift
16	  local cxx="${CXX:-g++}"
17	  local std="${CPPSTD:-c++23}"
18	  local exe="/tmp/${USER}-cppr-$(basename "${src%.*}")"
19	
20	  "$cxx" -std="$std" -O2 -pipe \
21	    -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion \
22	    "$src" -o "$exe" || return $?
23	
24	  "$exe" "$@"
25	}
26	
27	# Debug/sanitize build: cpprd main.cpp [program args...]
28	cpprd() {
29	  if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
30	    echo "usage: cpprd <file.cpp> [args...]"
31	    return 2
32	  fi
33	
34	  local src="$1"; shift
35	  local cxx="${CXX:-g++}"
36	  local std="${CPPSTD:-c++23}"
37	  local exe="/tmp/${USER}-cpprd-$(basename "${src%.*}")"
38	
39	  "$cxx" -std="$std" -O0 -g3 -pipe \
40	    -Wall -Wextra -Wpedantic -Wshadow -Wconversion -Wsign-conversion \
41	    -fsanitize=address,undefined -fno-omit-frame-pointer \
42	    "$src" -o "$exe" || return $?
43	
44	  "$exe" "$@"
45	}
46	
47	
48	source /usr/share/cachyos-zsh-config/cachyos-config.zsh
49	
50	alias "stremio"="flatpak run com.stremio.Stremio"
51	alias nvchad='NVIM_APPNAME="nvchad" nvim'
52	
53	alias "nvim"="bob run nightly"
54	export EDITOR="bob run nightly"
55	
56	export PYENV_ROOT="$HOME/.pyenv"
57	[[ -d $PYENV_ROOT/bin ]] && export PATH="$PYENV_ROOT/bin:$PATH"
58	eval "$(pyenv init -)"
59	
60	export PATH="/home/garrosh/.bun/bin:$PATH"
61	export PATH="$HOME/bin:$PATH"
62	# To customize prompt, run `p10k configure` or edit ~/.p10k.zsh.
63	[[ ! -f ~/.p10k.zsh ]] || source ~/.p10k.zsh
64	
65	export W3CHAMP='export WINEPREFIX="$HOME/Faugus/battlenet"
66	wine "C:\Program Files\W3Champions\W3Champions.exe"'
67	export PATH="$HOME/.local/bin:$PATH"
68

content.text: Your `.zshrc` uses Powerlevel10k (p10k) rather than a standard PS1. I'll read your p10k configuration to extract the prompt elements.

content.content:
1	# ~/.p10k.zsh
2	# Based on your current p10k-pure config, recolored to match naysayer.nvim.
3	
4	# Temporarily change options.
5	'builtin' 'local' '-a' 'p10k_config_opts'
6	[[ ! -o 'aliases'         ]] || p10k_config_opts+=('aliases')
7	[[ ! -o 'sh_glob'         ]] || p10k_config_opts+=('sh_glob')
8	[[ ! -o 'no_brace_expand' ]] || p10k_config_opts+=('no_brace_expand')
9	'builtin' 'setopt' 'no_aliases' 'no_sh_glob' 'brace_expand'
10	
11	() {
12	  emulate -L zsh -o extended_glob
13	
14	  # Unset all configuration options.
15	  unset -m '(POWERLEVEL9K_*|DEFAULT_USER)~POWERLEVEL9K_GITSTATUS_DIR'
16	
17	  # Zsh >= 5.1 is required.
18	  [[ $ZSH_VERSION == (5.<1->*|<6->.*) ]] || return
19	
20	  # Naysayer palette (hex from your nvim theme).
21	  local bg='#062625'
22	  local fg='#d0b892'
23	  local comment='#53d549'
24	  local punct='#8cde94'
25	  local keyword='#ffffff'
26	  local string='#3ad0b5'
27	  local constant='#87ffde'
28	  local blue='#66D9EF'
29	  local violet='#AE81FF'
30	  local orange='#FD971F'
31	  local warn='#ffaa00'
32	  local err='#ff0000'
33	  local linefg='#126367'
34	
35	  # Left prompt segments.
36	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_LEFT_PROMPT_ELEMENTS=(
37	    # =========================[ Line #1 ]=========================
38	    context
39	    dir
40	    vcs
41	    command_execution_time
42	    # =========================[ Line #2 ]=========================
43	    newline
44	    virtualenv
45	    prompt_char
46	  )
47	
48	  # Right prompt segments.
49	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_RIGHT_PROMPT_ELEMENTS=(
50	    # =========================[ Line #1 ]=========================
51	    time
52	    # =========================[ Line #2 ]=========================
53	    newline
54	  )
55	
56	  # Basic style options (keep "pure" look).
57	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_BACKGROUND=                            # transparent background
58	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_{LEFT,RIGHT}_{LEFT,RIGHT}_WHITESPACE=  # no surrounding whitespace
59	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_{LEFT,RIGHT}_SUBSEGMENT_SEPARATOR=' '
60	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_{LEFT,RIGHT}_SEGMENT_SEPARATOR=
61	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VISUAL_IDENTIFIER_EXPANSION=
62	
63	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_ADD_NEWLINE=true
64	
65	  # Prompt symbol colors.
66	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_CHAR_OK_{VIINS,VICMD,VIVIS}_FOREGROUND=$punct
67	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_CHAR_ERROR_{VIINS,VICMD,VIVIS}_FOREGROUND=$err
68	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_CHAR_{OK,ERROR}_VIINS_CONTENT_EXPANSION='λ'
69	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_CHAR_{OK,ERROR}_VICMD_CONTENT_EXPANSION='λ'
70	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_CHAR_{OK,ERROR}_VIVIS_CONTENT_EXPANSION='λ'
71	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_PROMPT_CHAR_OVERWRITE_STATE=false
72	
73	  # Virtualenv (subtle, like your "dimmed" feel).
74	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VIRTUALENV_FOREGROUND=$linefg
75	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VIRTUALENV_SHOW_PYTHON_VERSION=false
76	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VIRTUALENV_{LEFT,RIGHT}_DELIMITER=
77	
78	  # Directory (match your blue accents).
79	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_DIR_FOREGROUND=$blue
80	
81	  # Context format:
82	  # - root: user in keyword(white), @host in linefg
83	  # - normal: user@host in linefg
84	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_CONTEXT_ROOT_TEMPLATE="%F{$keyword}%n%f%F{$linefg}@%m%f"
85	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_CONTEXT_TEMPLATE="%F{$linefg}%n@%m%f"
86	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_CONTEXT_{DEFAULT,SUDO}_CONTENT_EXPANSION=
87	
88	  # Command duration.
89	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_COMMAND_EXECUTION_TIME_THRESHOLD=5
90	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_COMMAND_EXECUTION_TIME_PRECISION=0
91	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_COMMAND_EXECUTION_TIME_FORMAT='d h m s'
92	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_COMMAND_EXECUTION_TIME_FOREGROUND=$warn
93	
94	  # Git (base is "linefg"; dirty/warn/error use naysayer accents).
95	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_FOREGROUND=$linefg
96	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_LOADING_TEXT=
97	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_MAX_SYNC_LATENCY_SECONDS=0
98	
99	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_{INCOMING,OUTGOING}_CHANGESFORMAT_FOREGROUND=$constant
100	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_GIT_HOOKS=(vcs-detect-changes git-untracked git-aheadbehind)
101	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_BRANCH_ICON=
102	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_COMMIT_ICON='@'
103	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_{STAGED,UNSTAGED,UNTRACKED}_ICON=
104	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_DIRTY_ICON='*'
105	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_INCOMING_CHANGES_ICON=':⇣'
106	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_OUTGOING_CHANGES_ICON=':⇡'
107	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_{COMMITS_AHEAD,COMMITS_BEHIND}_MAX_NUM=1
108	
109	  # Colorize VCS content depending on state (Pure-like, but with your palette).
110	  # Note: p10k doesn't have per-state vars in this pure preset, so we use content formatting:
111	  # - clean stays $linefg
112	  # - dirty (*) becomes $warn
113	  # - untracked (if hook outputs it) gets $orange via the same formatter.
114	  #
115	  # Also keep the original "no spaces" cleanup.
116	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_CONTENT_EXPANSION='${${${P9K_CONTENT/⇣* :⇡/⇣⇡}// }//:/ }'
117	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_MODIFIED_FOREGROUND=$warn
118	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_UNTRACKED_FOREGROUND=$orange
119	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_VCS_CONFLICTED_FOREGROUND=$err
120	
121	  # Time (subtle foreground, readable on bg).
122	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_TIME_FOREGROUND=$fg
123	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_TIME_FORMAT='%D{%H:%M:%S}'
124	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_TIME_UPDATE_ON_COMMAND=false
125	
126	  # Transient + instant prompt (keep your choices).
127	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_TRANSIENT_PROMPT=always
128	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_INSTANT_PROMPT=verbose
129	
130	  typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_DISABLE_HOT_RELOAD=true
131	
132	  (( ! $+functions[p10k] )) || p10k reload
133	}
134	
135	typeset -g POWERLEVEL9K_CONFIG_FILE=${${(%):-%x}:a}
136	
137	(( ${#p10k_config_opts} )) && setopt ${p10k_config_opts[@]}
138	'builtin' 'unset' 'p10k_config_opts'
139	
140

content.text: Your p10k prompt includes: user@host, current directory, git status, and time. I'll create a status line script that mirrors these elements using the Claude Code session data where possible, and shell commands for the rest.

content.content: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /home/garrosh.

toolUseResult: Error: File does not exist. Note: your current working directory is /home/garrosh.

content.text: Now I'll create the status line script and update the settings.

input.new_string:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Claude Code status line — mirrors your p10k prompt
# Elements: user@host  cwd  git  time | model  context%

input=$(cat)

cwd=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.workspace.current_dir // .cwd // ""')
model=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.model.display_name // ""')
used_pct=$(echo "$input" | jq -r '.context_window.used_percentage // empty')

# user@host (linefg #126367)
user_host=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s@%s\033[0m' "$(whoami)" "$(hostname -s)")

# cwd (blue #66D9EF) — abbreviate $HOME to ~
display_cwd="${cwd/#$HOME/\~}"
dir_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;102;217;239m%s\033[0m' "$display_cwd")

# git info (linefg / warn on dirty)
git_part=""
if git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --git-dir > /dev/null 2>&1; then
  branch=$(git -C "$cwd" symbolic-ref --short HEAD 2>/dev/null || git -C "$cwd" rev-parse --short HEAD 2>/dev/null)
  if [ -n "$branch" ]; then
    # check dirty
    if ! git -C "$cwd" diff --quiet 2>/dev/null || ! git -C "$cwd" diff --cached --quiet 2>/dev/null; then
      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[38;2;255;170;0m*\033[0m' "$branch")
    else
      git_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$branch")
    fi
  fi
fi

# time (fg #d0b892)
time_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;208;184;146m%s\033[0m' "$(date +%H:%M:%S)")

# model (subtle)
model_part=""
[ -n "$model" ] && model_part=$(printf '\033[38;2;18;99;103m%s\033[0m' "$model")

# context usage
ctx_part=""
if [ -n "$used_pct" ]; then
  used_int=$(printf '%.0f' "$used_pct")
  if [ "$used_int" -ge 80 ]; then
    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;255;0;0mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
  elif [ "$used_int" -ge 50 ]; then
    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;255;170;0mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
  else
    ctx_part=$(printf ' \033[38;2;18;99;103mctx:%d%%\033[0m' "$used_int")
  fi
fi

# Compose line
line="${user_host}  ${dir_part}${git_part}  ${time_part}"
[ -n "$model_part" ] && line="${line}  ${model_part}${ctx_part}"

printf '%s\n' "$line"

content.content: The file /home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh has been updated successfully.
The file /home/garrosh/.claude/settings.json has been updated successfully.

content.text:
Done. Here is a summary of what was configured:

**Script created:** `/home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh`

**Settings updated:** `/home/garrosh/.claude/settings.json`

The status line mirrors your p10k prompt elements using the same Naysayer color palette:

- `user@host` in linefg (`#126367`)
- Current working directory in blue (`#66D9EF`), with `~` abbreviation for home
- Git branch in linefg, with a `*` suffix in warn orange (`#ffaa00`) when the working tree is dirty
- Current time (`HH:MM:SS`) in fg (`#d0b892`)
- Claude model name in linefg
- Context usage percentage, color-coded: linefg below 50%, orange from 50-79%, red at 80%+

The prompt character (`λ`) and virtualenv segment were omitted since those are interactive shell concepts that don't apply to the Claude Code status line.

---

For any future changes to the status line — such as adding rate limit display, adjusting colors, or reordering segments — ask Claude to use the **statusline-setup** agent again, which will update `/home/garrosh/.claude/statusline-command.sh` and `/home/garrosh/.claude/settings.json` accordingly.
